David L. Call papers, 1977-1995.
Collection Number: 21-2-1620

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
David L. Call papers, 1977-1995.
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
21-2-1620
Abstract:
Records of Dean David L. Call include materials pertaining to various departments of the college; other colleges at Cornell University; the Agricultural Experiment Station in Geneva, New York; the Cornell Board of Trustees and administration; Cornell Plantations; SUNY; CALS Alumni Association; Isles of Shoals; the Sea Grant Institute; Boyce Thompson Institute; New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets; New York State Council of Farmer Cooperatives; the Council of Agricultural Organizations; Agway, Inc., Agricultural Resources Commission; and the Environmental Protection Agency.
Creator:
Call, David L., 1932-
Quanitities:
68.6 cubic feet.
Language:
Collection material in English
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE

Dean, New York State College of Agriculture and Life Sciences (CALS), 1977-1995.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION

Records of Dean David L. Call include materials pertaining to various departments of the college; other colleges at Cornell University; the Agricultural Experiment Station in Geneva, New York; the Cornell Board of Trustees and administration; Cornell Plantations; SUNY; CALS Alumni Association; Isles of Shoals; the Sea Grant Institute; Boyce Thompson Institute; New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets; New York State Council of Farmer Cooperatives; the Council of Agricultural Organizations; Agway, Inc., Agricultural Resources Commission; and the Environmental Protection Agency. Included are minutes, reports, statements, correspondence, speeches and other items concerning pesticide use and environmental issues, bovine hormone use, agricultural extension, rural development, pest control, migrant labor, agricultural policies, soil and water conservation, legislative bills, investments, land-grant affairs, academic funding and tuition, development, budgets, admissions, faculty salaries and tenure, staff relations, affirmative action and minority affairs, sex discrimination, education programs, libraries, computers, campus planning and development, university policies and procedures, and other matters. Major correspondents include Frank Rhodes, Henry Uihlein, and Roy Carver. Also photographs and items relating to his retirement. Also materials relating to the Unimal and a Chinese vase presented to Call by President Lee Teng-hui of Taiwan.
SEPARATED MATERIAL

Correspondence with George A. West regarding Theta Alpha fraternity, Cornell Beta Chapter, including a list of early members added to the Theta Alpha collection, #37-4-2196. Biographical information, articles, and a photograph added to faculty biographical file, #47-10-3394.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

David L. Call papers, #21-2-1620. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Access Restrictions:

Access to Boxes 1-67 restricted to the permission of the Dean's Office, New York State College of Agriculture and Life Sciences.

Box 68 Folder 1
Mater Farmers of America
 November 8, 1938
Scope and Contents
Includes and list of Master Farmers in New York State from the Master Farmers of America.
Box 68 Folder 2
Photographs
Scope and Contents
Includes images of former Taiwan President Lee Teng-hui, Richard Lockwood, Mario Cuomo, Hank Dullea, William Parment, and others.
Box 68 Folder 3
China trip correspondence
 March 1980
Scope and Contents
Information regarding 1980 Cornell delegation trip to China and Call's letters home.
Box 68 Folder 4
Recognitions
Scope and Contents
Includes Marquis Who's Who, Cornell University Council, Liberty Hyde Bailey Leadership Society also a poem from Robert C. Woodbury.
Box 68
Retirement party photo album
 1995
Box 68
Your Evening At The Crown Jewel Of The College
 June 7, 1995
Scope and Contents
Photo album of retirement event at the New York State Agricultural Experiment Station, Geneva.
Box 68
Reflections, Volume I
 June 10, 1995
Scope and Contents
Testimonials, letters, and other thanks and reminisces in recognition of Dean Call's retirement from his friends and colleagues.
Box 68
Reflections, Volume II
 June 10, 1995
Scope and Contents
Testimonials, letters, and other thanks and reminisces, in recognition of Dean Call's retirement from his friends and colleagues.
Box 69 MU-1741-1742
Howard Babcock Unimal toy (wooden frame removed), Unimal coffee mug with Agway tag, and a Unimal advertisement
MU-1743
Chinese vase presented to Call by President Lee Teng-hui of Taiwan.
 1995
